regime_fall.current_status: Iran and the US stand on the brink of ceasefire collapse as military escalations intensify—with the IRGC intercepting tankers and threatening destruction, while Iran's government signals hardline resistance through military rhetoric and grave desecration of protest victims, even as diplomatic channels attempt to reconvene in Islamabad on April 21 before the ceasefire formally expires.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Iran's IRGC intercepted multiple tankers in the Strait of Hormuz on April 20, fired warning shots at an Indian-flagged vessel threatening to destroy it, and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf declared Iran will not negotiate under US threats while claiming to have prepared 'new options on the battlefield,' escalating confrontation as the ceasefire deadline approaches.
